message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Mr. Lyons Steakhouse (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 797 reviews; Tripadvisor 281 reviews; OpenTable ~1,888 diner reviews — aggregate roughly 2,500-3,000 across major platforms
- Sentiment: mostly positive but trending mixed
- Longevity: Building operated as Lyons English Grille from 1945; purchased by Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar) in 2014, renovated and reopened as Mr. Lyons in 2015 — approximately 11 years under current concept, stable F10 ownership
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life — Editors' Pick Best Steakhouse 2021","Palm Springs Life — Editors' Pick Best Steakhouse 2022","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award (top 10% of properties)","Operated under Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar); Lazar has a James Beard Foundation chef profile [UNVERIFIED whether Mr. Lyons itself carries a James Beard nomination]"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably on narrow steakhouse-specific queries ('steakhouse Palm Springs', 'best steakhouse Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs supper club'). Does NOT surface in broader regional queries ('best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'best fine dining Coachella Valley 2025') where agents return Mastro's, The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, The Edge, Eddie V's, Morgan's, Wally's Desert Turtle instead. Visibility is anchored to the Palm Springs Life editor pick and to platform-listing presence (Yelp/Tripadvisor/OpenTable) rather than to recent editorial coverage
- Site structured data: [UNVERIFIED] The owned domain mrlyonsf10.com returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch, blocking direct inspection of schema.org markup, hours object, or menu structure. Bot-blocking itself is an agent-readability negative signal — an agent crawler hitting the same 403 cannot extract hours, menu, or location data from the canonical source and must fall back to third-party aggregators
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"A little history - Mr. Lyons Steakhouse | Steak House | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.mrlyonsf10.com/about"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/mr-lyons-palm-springs"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse | Palm Springs, California, United States | Venue Report","url":"https://www.venuereport.com/venue/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse – COACHELLA MAGAZINE","url":"https://coachellamagazine.com/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"*Homepage - Mr. Lyons Steakhouse | Steak House | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.mrlyonsf10.com/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/mr-lyons-steakhouse/58/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons - Steakhouse Restaurant in Palm Springs, CA | The Vendry","url":"https://thevendry.com/venue/110825/mr.-lyons-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"Palm Springs Restaurants in Historic Buildings | Where to Dine","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/palm-springs-restaurants-in-iconic-buildings/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons : Palm Springs | Xtreme Foodies - The world's Essential Eats curated by local food experts","url":"https://www.xtremefoodies.com/north-america/Palm%20Springs/review/Mr.-Lyons/Mr.-Lyons/9060_9336"},{"title":"Dinner - Luxsy Palm Springs","url":"https://luxsypalmsprings.com/area-guide/dinner/"}]

Based on the search results, here's the history of Mr. Lyons Steakhouse in Palm Springs:

## Original Opening

The restaurant was originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945. It was established by David Lyons as a family-owned steakhouse that delighted diners for over 70 years, and was inspired by the glamour of classic supper clubs.

## Historical Significance

Lyons English Grille has roots in Palm Springs' prewar resort years, in an era before the city's full Hollywood explosion, when it was already becoming a sophisticated winter destination for affluent travelers. The establishment caught the attention of classic, old Hollywood names, including Frank Sinatra, Marilyn Monroe, and Bob Hope.

## Modern Renovation and Rebrand

In 2014 the building was purchased from David Lyons, and after a complete renovation, the restaurant reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons, a homage to the original owner. The year-long meticulous renovation resulted in Mr. Lyons Steakhouse being born in 2015, paying homage to David Lyons himself.

Today, Mr. Lyons operates as a modern steakhouse that honors its historic roots while offering contemporary cuisine and service.

Web search results for query: "Mr. Lyons Palm Springs Tara Lazar Foundation 10 Creative 2015"

Links: [{"title":"Interview: Tara Lazar (Foundation 10 Creative, Palm Springs)","url":"https://foodgps.com/interview-chef-tara-lazar-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse Reimagines Iconic 1940s Lyons English Grille in Palm Springs | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/mr-lyons-steakhouse-reimagines-iconic-1940s-lyons-english-grille-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"F10 Creative | LinkedIn","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/company/f10-creative"},{"title":"F10 Creative - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/tag/f10-creative/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons | Palm Springs Venue | PartySlate","url":"https://www.partyslate.com/venues/mr-lyons"},{"title":"F10 Creative | The Org","url":"https://theorg.com/org/f10-creative"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/tag/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"Jesse Bluma at Pointe Viven: Where to Toast and Dine: Mister Lyons and Seymour’s Review by Jesse Bluma at Pointe Viven","url":"http://pointeviven.blogspot.com/2017/07/Where-to-Toast-and-Dine-Mister-Lyons-Mr-David-Lyons-Seymours-Review-Jesse-Bluma-Pointe-Viven-Zomato-Number-1-One-Blog-Tara-Lazar-Foundation-10.html"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mr. Lyons, Palm Springs, Tara Lazar Foundation 10 Creative, and 2015:

Foundation 10 Creative updated iconic Lyons English Grille, which now goes by Mr. Lyons. Foundation 10 Creative took over the South Palm Springs space from the original owner, David Lyons, and reopened as Mr. Lyons Steakhouse in May. Reimagined in 2015 by Tara Lazar, Mr. Lyons is the newest offering from the Foundation 10 family, taking the same attention to detail we've brought to Cheeky's, Birba and Alcazar to a new vista on the South end of town.

F10 Creative is a collective owned by restaurateur and hotelier, Tara Lazar, the local force behind well-known Palm Springs restaurants, Cheeky's and Birba, as well as Alcazar hotel. Executive Chef Chris Hora put his own stamp on things, noting a variety of international influences like Asian, North African and Latin, plus a couple of Chinese techniques blended with California influence.

content.text: The Tripadvisor search didn't confirm a Travelers' Choice award; rankings of #82-101 of 281 restaurants in Palm Springs don't suggest top 10% status. Let me verify directly.

input.prompt: Does this listing show a Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award badge? What is the current ranking among Palm Springs restaurants and the review count?

input.query: "Tara Lazar" Palm Springs chef biography James Beard Foundation profile

content.content:
# Mr. Lyons Restaurant - Tripadvisor Information

**Travelers' Choice Award:** No, Mr. Lyons does not display a Travelers' Choice badge on its listing.

**Current Ranking & Reviews:** The restaurant is ranked "#81 of 281 Restaurants in Palm Springs" with a rating of 3.9 out of 5 bubbles based on "281 reviews."

The page notes that Travelers' Choice awards go to establishments that "consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor," which Mr. Lyons does not appear to qualify for at its current ranking position.

Web search results for query: ""Tara Lazar" Palm Springs chef biography James Beard Foundation profile"

Links: [{"title":"Tara Lazar | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/chef/tara-lazar"},{"title":"Tara Lazar Joins the Palm Desert Food and Wine Dinner at the James Beard House in NYC — THE F(10)WORD","url":"https://thefword.blog/posts/2019/9/26/james-beard-dinner-2019"},{"title":"James Beard House Feast Can Be Found at Palm Desert Food & Wine","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/james-beard-house/"},{"title":"Palm Desert Food and Wine 2019 | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/events/palm-desert-food-and-wine-2019"},{"title":"Interview: Tara Lazar (Foundation 10 Creative, Palm Springs)","url":"https://foodgps.com/interview-chef-tara-lazar-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Tara Lazar - San Diego Food + Wine Festival™","url":"https://www.sandiegowineclassic.com/our-talent/tara-lazar/"},{"title":"Tara Lazar Archives - Food GPS","url":"https://foodgps.com/tag/tara-lazar/"},{"title":"Tara Lazar — Snacky Tunes","url":"https://www.snackytunes.space/cmm/2018/7/16/tara-lazar"},{"title":"Palm Springs Life's 40 Under 40 Selection - TARA LAZAR ...","url":"https://www.facebook.com/video/video.php?v=1586351908823"},{"title":"Tara Lazar - Founder at F10 Creative | LinkedIn","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/in/tara-lazar-20045219b/"}]

Here's what I found about Tara Lazar, the Palm Springs chef, from the James Beard Foundation and other sources:

## Biography

Tara Lazar is a self-trained chef who's carved out quite a niche in Palm Springs with Foundation 10 Creative's diverse roster. Tara grew up in Palm Springs, and left for a tennis scholarship at UC Berkeley, which led to a career in finance in San Francisco. She was a stock trader during her 20s, moved back to Palm Springs and was annoyed there wasn't any good breakfast.

## Restaurant Portfolio

She started with popular breakfast spot Cheeky's in 2008 and has since added Italian-inspired Birba and a boutique hotel called Alcazar on the same block. F10 also runs pan-Latin Chi Chi at the Avalon Palm Springs, and her group recently updated iconic Lyons English Grille, which now goes by Mr. Lyons.

## James Beard Foundation Recognition

Chef Tara Lazar is listed on the James Beard Foundation website with her restaurants Cheeky's, Birba, Mr. Lyons Steakhouse, and F10 Catering + Events in Palm Springs, CA. F10 Creative founder Tara Lazar was invited to join other chefs at the Palm Desert Food and Wine Dinner at the Beard House in New York City.

content.text: The PSL 2021/2022 Editors' Pick claim appears on Mr. Lyons' own marketing/OpenTable/Vendry pages but I cannot independently verify on the PSL website (403). I'll mark as partially confirmed (echoed by venue's own listings, not primary verified).

confirms: Longevity claim: Building operated as Lyons English Grille from 1945; purchased by Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar) in 2014, renovated and reopened as Mr. Lyons in 2015 — independently confirmed via multiple sources including Palm Springs Life, Food GPS, Coachella Magazine, and venue's own about page
Stable Foundation 10 / Tara Lazar ownership confirmed (also operates Cheeky's, Birba, Alcazar hotel, Chi Chi)
Tara Lazar has a James Beard Foundation chef profile at jamesbeard.org/chef/tara-lazar — confirmed (profile exists, lists Mr. Lyons among her venues)
Yelp review count of 797 confirmed via Yelp listing snippet
Tripadvisor review count of 281 confirmed via Tripadvisor listing
contradicts: Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award (top 10% of properties) — REFUTED. Direct fetch of the Tripadvisor listing shows NO Travelers' Choice badge. Mr. Lyons is currently ranked #81 of 281 restaurants in Palm Springs (roughly the 29th percentile), well outside the top 10% threshold required for the award. The claim should be removed.
flagged_as_unverified: Palm Springs Life Editors' Pick Best Steakhouse 2021 AND 2022 — the specific year attribution (2021 and 2022) is echoed on Mr. Lyons' own marketing (OpenTable bio, Vendry listing), but the primary palmspringslife.com 'Best of the Best Steakhouse' article URL returned HTTP 403 to automated verification, so the specific years could not be independently confirmed at the source. A PSL Editors' Pick clearly exists, but the 2021/2022 specificity rests on self-reported marketing copy.

James Beard nomination for Mr. Lyons itself — no evidence of a JBF nomination for the restaurant or its chef in semifinalist/finalist lists. Tara Lazar's JBF presence is a chef-directory profile and a 2019 Palm Desert Food & Wine Beard House guest-chef appearance (an invitation, not an awards nomination). The brief's existing UNVERIFIED flag on this is correct and should remain.
OpenTable ~1,888 diner reviews — not independently checked in this lens (review-volume verification is outside credentials-and-history scope)
input.notes: History/longevity claims are solid and well-sourced. The Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award claim is the clearest failure — it's demonstrably false at the current ranking and there's no badge on the listing; recommend striking it from credentials. The Tara Lazar JBF profile is real but is a chef-directory entry plus a 2019 guest-chef Beard House appearance, NOT an awards nomination — the existing UNVERIFIED flag on Mr. Lyons carrying a JB nomination is appropriate and should stay. PSL Editors' Pick clearly exists in some form but the 2021/2022 year specificity is sourced only from the venue's own marketing copy echoed by aggregators, not independently confirmed at palmspringslife.com (which 403s).
